info.title: Rigbox API
info.description: Manage workspaces, apps, snapshots, tools, and more on the Rigbox platform.

get.summary: List all available catalog apps.

200.description: List of available catalog app items

get.summary: List all apps for the authenticated user.

post.summary: Create a new app.

get.summary: Get an app by ID.

Check if an app's URL is live by verifying actual content is served,
and probe the underlying service and port status.
get.description:
Returns `live` (HTTP reachable), `service_status` (service state),
and `port_open` (whether the port is bound).

get.summary: List log sources for an app.

post.summary: Create a log source for an app.

get.summary: Fetch logs for an app.
Stream app logs via Server-Sent Events.
Get app time-series metrics.
Get app summary metrics.
Get configurable parameters for an app.

patch.summary: Update configurable parameters for an app.

post.summary: Restart an app's service.
Start an app's service.
Stop an app's service.
POST /api/v1/apps/:id/verify-domain - re-check DNS for the app's custom domain.

get.summary: List exact deployable catalog refs for the current user.

200.description: Versioned official and community catalog items

get.summary: Get one exact deployable catalog ref for the current user.
List published community workspace compositions the current user can see.
get.description:
Returns the caller's own items first (any visibility), then community-visible
items filtered by the caller's `community_items_preference`.

post.summary: Publish a private or Community workspace composition version.

delete.summary:
Unpublish a community composition version. Only the owning vendor can delete.
Built-in compositions (`vendor=rigbox`) cannot be deleted.

post.summary: Deprecate a community composition version. Only the owning vendor can mutate it.
Yank a community composition version. Only the owning vendor can mutate it.

get.summary: Get the current user's community install record for an installed app.
Get the current user's community profile and catalog preference.

put.summary: Update the current user's community username or catalog preference.

get.summary: List published community app recipes the current user can see.
get.description:
Returns the user's own items first (any visibility) then community-visible
items filtered by the caller's `community_items_preference`.

post.summary: Publish a private or Community app recipe version.

delete.summary:
Unpublish a community recipe version. Only the owning vendor can delete.
Built-in recipes (`vendor=rigbox`) cannot be deleted.

post.summary: Deprecate a community recipe version. Only the owning vendor can mutate it.
Yank a community recipe version. Only the owning vendor can mutate it.

get.summary: Get aggregate community registry health and adoption metrics.

post.summary: Promote exact community recipe versions to Verified.
post.description:
This endpoint is intended for the future public `rigbox-dev/registry`
repository CI/webhook flow. The request body is a normalized allowlist
delta, signed with GitHub-style `X-Hub-Signature-256`.

parameters.name: x-hub-signature-256
parameters.description: sha256=<hex HMAC of the raw body>

get.summary: Get composite dashboard data.
get.description:
Fetches workspaces, apps, limits, and AI credits in parallel and returns
them in a single JSON response.

200.description: Dashboard data including workspaces, apps, limits, and AI credits

get.summary: Preview an exact Community workspace composition ref.
Preview an exact Official, private owner, or allowed community deploy ref.
Get feature flags resolved for the calling user.
get.description:
The response is a flat `{ "flag_name": true/false }` map where access is
pre-resolved using the caller's email and plan. The frontend caches this
at startup and checks keys with `isFeatureOn('flag_name')`.

200.description: Feature flags for the calling user

post.summary: Create a workspace from a template.

get.summary: Get all roadmap vote counts.
get.description:
If an Authorization header is present, also returns the user's own votes.
No auth required - anyone can see vote counts.

200.description: Roadmap vote counts and optional user votes

post.summary: Toggle your vote on a roadmap feature.

parameters.name: feature_key
parameters.description: Feature key to toggle vote on

get.summary: List all service specs.

post.summary: Create a new service spec.

get.summary: Get a service spec by ID.
List all setup scripts.

post.summary: Create a new setup script.

get.summary: Get a setup script by ID.
List all snapshots for the authenticated user.

post.summary: Restore a workspace from a snapshot.

get.summary: List SSH keys for the authenticated user.

post.summary:
Add an SSH key to your account.
The key is automatically synced to all running workspaces.

delete.summary:
Delete an SSH key from your account.
The key is automatically removed from all running workspaces.

get.summary: List available workspace templates.
Get account-level AI defaults.

put.summary: Update AI mode for the authenticated user.

get.summary: Get AI usage history and workspace breakdown.
Get account AI usage metric series.
Get account AI usage metric summary.
Get AI credit balance for the authenticated user.
Get effective account limits and current usage.
List available regions.
Get user settings.
List all workspaces for the authenticated user.

post.summary: Create a new workspace.
Deploy a workspace composition (built-in or community) end-to-end.

get.summary: Get a workspace by ID.
Get the AI configuration for a workspace.
Get workspace AI usage metric series.
Get workspace AI usage metric summary.

post.summary: Launch an exact Official, private owner, or allowed community deploy ref into a workspace.

Update environment variables for a workspace.
Changes take effect immediately if the workspace is running.
Detect or expose a port as a routable app.
Install a catalog app into a workspace.

get.summary: Poll the status of an async app install job.
List ports with active listeners in a workspace.
get.description:
Detect which ports are listening in the workspace.
Cross-checks against existing apps to flag already-exposed ports.

get.summary: Fetch structured logs from a workspace.
Stream workspace logs via Server-Sent Events.
Get live resource metrics for a workspace.
Get workspace resource metric series.
Get workspace resource metric summary.
List all workspace services with health status.
Get a single workspace service by name.
Fetch logs for a specific workspace service by name.
List snapshots for a workspace.

post.summary: Create a snapshot of a workspace.
Restore a workspace from a snapshot.

get.summary: List SSH keys currently in the workspace.

post.summary: Sync all account SSH keys to the workspace.
Start a workspace.
Stop a workspace.

get.summary: List tasks for a workspace.

put.summary: Sync tasks from td running inside a workspace VM.

get.summary: List available tools and their status for a workspace.

200.description: List of available workspace tools with status

post.summary: Install a tool asynchronously.
post.description: Returns a `job_id` for polling install progress.

get.summary: Poll the status of an async tool install job.

post.summary: Launch a tool in the workspace.

get.summary: Get the status of a specific tool.

post.summary: Stop a running tool.

ApiErrorBody.description: JSON body returned for all errors.

configurable_params.description:
User-configurable parameters for this app.
Values are submitted at install time and resolved via `{PARAM_<KEY>}`
placeholders in install scripts, exec_start, and config files.

destructive_delete.description:
If true, deletion destroys user-modifiable files (e.g. scaffolded starter code).
Requires `?force_delete=true` and the UI renders extra confirmation
(type app name or check a warning box).

incompatible_templates.description:
Template IDs that already include this app (e.g. `["chat-app-starter"]`).
Installing on workspaces created from these templates is blocked.

min_disk_mb.description: Minimum disk space hint (MB). Informational only - not enforced.

min_ram_mb.description: Minimum RAM hint (MB). Informational only - not enforced.

port.description: Internal port the app listens on.

preview.description: Whether this item is in preview (feature-flagged).

system_tags.description:
Author-declared discovery tags. Merged into the auto-generated
`["catalog:{id}-{ws}", "catalog"]` tags on the installed app.

live.description: Whether the app responded to an HTTP probe.

port_drift.description: True when the service is active but the expected port is not listening.

port_open.description: Whether the expected port is listening inside the workspace.

specs.description: The parameter schema (snapshotted at creation time).

values.description: Current values (from metadata.config). Sensitive values are masked.

CompositionChildInstall.description: One child install handle returned to the client so the UI can poll status.

configurable_params.description:
Configurable parameters this child app exposes. The deploy form uses
these to render per-app `ParamFields` editors when launching the
composition into a workspace.

credentials_declared.description:
True if this child declares default credentials (server-generated or
pre-filled). Surfaces a "credentials required" hint in the deploy form.

managed.description: True if the recipe opts into Rigbox's managed AI proxy when installed.

oneOf.description: Floor minimums sourced from the base template (used by client UI as floors).

expected_apps.description: Apps pre-installed in the base template image (only for built-in compositions).

image.description:
Final image the workspace boots from (e.g. "base", "firecrawl").
Lifted from `manifest.workspace.image` so the catalog UI can show
the actual VM image without reading the raw manifest.

image_bound.description:
True when this composition's apps/services live inside `image`
rather than being installed via app recipes at provision time.
Image-bound compositions (e.g. firecrawl) start faster but the
manifest can't fully describe what's inside the image.

log_sources.description:
Log source presets shipped with the base template (file paths,
systemd units) for the workspace-level Logs panel.

manifest_json.description: Raw manifest JSON for the "View manifest" affordance.

portability_notes.description:
Author-declared notes describing what the image carries that the
manifest can't fully describe. Only populated when image_bound.

service_specs.description: Available systemd service specs shipped with the base template.

setup_scripts.description: Available setup scripts shipped with the base template.

verify_binaries.description:
Binaries the workspace expects pre-installed in `image` (checked
at first boot via `which`). Lifted from
`manifest.template.verify_binaries` so the catalog UI can show
users what binaries the image carries.

workspace_services.description: Backing services (databases, brokers) provisioned by the base template.

app_params.description: Per-child param values keyed by the child's deploy_ref.

env_vars.description: Workspace environment variables to overlay on top of composition defaults.

name.description: Optional workspace name. Defaults to a generated id from the slug.

service_spec_ids.description: User-selected service specs from their library.

setup_script_ids.description: User-selected setup scripts from their library.

depends_on.description: Backing services this app expects on the workspace (community only).

deploy_ref.description: Canonical exact ref that will be installed.

managed.description: True if this recipe opts into Rigbox's managed AI proxy when installed.

manifest_json.description: Raw manifest JSON for the "View manifest" affordance. Community only.

system_tags.description: Discovery tags from the recipe manifest (community only).

usage_examples.description: Copy-pasteable usage examples shown in the catalog detail "Usage" tab.

DetectResult.description: Detection result for exposing ports - shows what was found before confirmation.

oneOf.description: Search for a file by name within $HOME.
Check if all listed binaries exist on PATH.

DiscoveryStrategy.description: How a tool discovers whether it's available in a workspace.

flags.description: Map of feature flag names to their resolved boolean values.

credentials.description: Credentials generated at install time (only for apps that require login).

job_id.description: Job ID for async install - frontend should poll GET /workspaces/{id}/jobs/{job_id}.

live_installed.description: If workspace is running, whether live install was attempted.

app_status.description: Final app status after finalization (only set once job completes)

params.description: User-supplied values for the resolved app's configurable params.

ListeningPort.description: A port detected as listening in a workspace.

LogLine.description: A single log line - used by workspace logs and app logs.

MessageResponse.description: Generic success envelope - used across all domains.

ParamSpec.description:
One configurable parameter declared by a template expected app or catalog item.

The `key` is used as the `{PARAM_<KEY>}` placeholder (upper-cased) in
install scripts, exec_start, and config_file JSON values.

description.description: Longer description / hint text shown below the field.

group.description: Visual group header for related params.

help_url.description: Help URL (e.g. docs for getting an API key).

order.description: Display order within the group (lower = higher).

param_type.description: Determines the UI element and basic validation.

required.description: If true, deploy is blocked when the value is empty.

sensitive.description: If true, render as password field and mask in API responses.

validation_pattern.description: Optional regex the submitted value must match.

ParamType.description: Data type for validation + UI element selection.

manifest_yaml.description: Optional original YAML text used for digesting and review display.
Optional original YAML text used for digesting and review display.

app_params.description:
User-supplied parameter values for template expected apps, keyed by app name.
E.g. `{ "gateway": { "ai_provider": "anthropic", "telegram_bot_token": "..." } }`

catalog_ids.description: Optional catalog app IDs to pre-install (Phase 3 composition).

service_spec_ids.description: User-selected service specs from their library. None keeps legacy default behavior.

setup_script_ids.description: User-selected setup scripts from their library. None keeps legacy default behavior.

expected_apps.description:
Expected apps created for this template (status=Installing).
Frontend should track these for install progress.

ServicePatchSpec.description: Specification for patching a systemd service with an EnvironmentFile.

env_file_path.description: Path to the env file to add (e.g. "/home/developer/.openclaw/.env").

remove_env_patterns.description: Environment= lines to remove if found (e.g. ["OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN"]).

ServiceSpec.description:
Account-level service spec that runs a background process inside workspaces.
Working directory is always /home/developer.

environment_file.description: Optional path to an environment file for the service.

systemd_unit_preview.description: Preview of the systemd unit that would be generated

SetupScript.description:
Account-level setup script for reproducible workspace environments.
Scripts run once on first boot or on every start.

SnapshotView.description: Read-only projection of a workspace snapshot.

SshKeyEntry.description: An SSH key entry from a workspace's authorized_keys.

configurable_params.description:
User-configurable parameters for this expected app.
Values are submitted at deploy time via `app_params` in QuickDeployRequest.

depends_on.description: Workspace services this app depends on (by name, e.g. "postgresql", "redis-server").

usage_examples.description:
Copy-pasteable API usage examples shown in the app detail "Usage" tab.
Placeholders: `{SUBDOMAIN}`, `{CRED_<key>}` - resolved client-side.

available.description: Whether the tool is now available after install

needs_install.description:
True if the tool requires setup before it can analyze/launch.
Discovery may still work but full functionality needs install.

status_sys.description:
Lifecycle state reported by the tool's background process.
Values are tool-defined (e.g. "analyzing", "installing", "building").
None when idle or no background process is running.

unavailable_reason.description: Brief explanation of why the tool is unavailable.

custom_domain.description: Custom domain (e.g. "api.example.com"). Set to empty string or null to remove.

community_items_preference.description: Community catalog visibility preference: disabled, verified_only, or enabled.

username.description: Claimable community username. Once claimed, username changes are blocked.

UsageExample.description: A copy-pasteable API usage example for a template app.

description.description: Optional description shown below the code block

event_id.description: Optional event ID. `X-GitHub-Delivery` is preferred when present.

WorkspaceService.description:
Runtime backing service instance tracked for health monitoring.
Distinct from ServiceSpec (which generates systemd units for custom processes).
WorkspaceService tracks apt-managed daemons like PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ.

coming_soon.description: If true, template is shown in UI but cannot be deployed yet.

env_file_copies.description: Copy .env to these additional paths at first boot.

has_tools.description: If true, this image ships the tools binary (ArchX, Virtual Browser, etc.).

image_bound.description:
True when this template's apps/services live inside the image
(e.g. firecrawl, openclaw) rather than being installed onto a
minimal base image at provision time. Mirrors the rule in
`domain::community::export::image_bound` so the catalog and
/templates UI can show the same `composable` / `image-bound`
distinction.

managed_ai_config.description: If true, this template supports managed AI mode (proxy + credits).

service_patches.description: Systemd services to patch with EnvironmentFile at first boot.

verify_binaries.description: Binaries to verify exist at first boot.

install_hint.description:
User-facing install command when the tool needs setup.
Shown in the UI when the tool is unavailable or partially available.

port.description: Port the tool listens on inside the VM.

tags.description: Tags indicating supported languages, runtimes, etc.
